mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive 20d returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales growth and CPI inflation indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually while RSAFS growth remains positive.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in > 55% of instances following positive INDPRO revisions, or the signal reverses direction during liquidity crunches.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index year-over-year growth surpasses 5.0%, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL exceeding 5.0% YoY, or the predictive power vanishes when real rates remain deeply negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust economic expansion without overheating, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 500k but UNRATE simultaneously rises above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which historically precede positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts rise but S&P 500 forward returns remain negative in > 60% of cases, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter change in real GDP confirms an economic contraction, triggering recession risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets rally following negative GDP prints, or the signal fails to predict negative returns when fiscal stimulus offsets the contraction.
